2605.00334.title: AgentFloor: How Far Up the tool use Ladder Can Small Open-Weight Models Go?
2605.00334.paper_summary: Production agentic systems make many model calls per user request, and most of those calls are short, structured, and routine. This raises a practical routing question that existing evaluations do not directly answer: which parts of an agent workflow truly require large frontier intelligence, and which can be handled by smaller models? We introduce AgentFloor, a deterministic 30-task benchmark organized as a six-tier capability ladder, spanning instruction following, tool use, multi-step coordination, and long-horizon planning under persistent constraints. We evaluate 16 open-weight models, from 0.27B to 32B parameters, alongside GPT-5 across 16,542 scored runs. Our results reveal a clear boundary of model necessity. Small and mid-sized open-weight models are already sufficient for much of the short-horizon, structured tool use work that dominates real agent pipelines, and in aggregate, the strongest open-weight model matches GPT-5 on our benchmark while being substantially cheaper and faster to run. The gap appears most clearly on long-horizon planning tasks that require sustained coordination and reliable constraint tracking over many steps, where frontier models still hold an advantage, though neither side reaches strong reliability. We also find that this boundary is not explained by scale alone: some failures respond to targeted interventions, but the effects are model-specific rather than universal. These findings suggest a practical design principle for agentic systems: use smaller open-weight models for the broad base of routine actions, and reserve large frontier models for the narrower class of tasks that truly demand deeper planning and control. We release the benchmark, harness, sweep configurations, and full run corpus.
2605.00334.paper_summary_zh: 生產代理系統在每個用戶請求中進行多次模型調用，而這些調用大多是短小、結構化且例行的。這引發了一個實際的路由問題，現有的評估並沒有直接回答：代理工作流程的哪些部分真正需要大型前沿智能，哪些可以由較小的模型處理？我們介紹了AgentFloor，一個確定性的30任務基準，組織為六層能力梯度，涵蓋指令遵循、工具使用、多步協調和在持續約束下的長期規劃。我們評估了16個開放權重模型，參數從0.27B到32B，並與GPT-5進行了16,542次計分運行的比較。我們的結果揭示了模型必要性的明確邊界。小型和中型開放權重模型已經足夠應對主導真實代理管道的許多短期、結構化的工具使用工作，並且在總體上，最強的開放權重模型在我們的基準上與GPT-5相匹配，同時運行成本和速度都顯著更低。這一差距在需要持續協調和可靠約束跟踪的長期規劃任務中最為明顯，前沿模型仍然佔有優勢，儘管雙方都未達到強可靠性。我們還發現，這一邊界並非僅由規模解釋：一些失敗對針對性干預有反應，但效果是模型特定的，而非普遍的。這些發現為代理系統提供了一個實用的設計原則：對於廣泛的例行行動，使用較小的開放權重模型，並將大型前沿模型保留給真正需要更深層次規劃和控制的狹窄任務類別。我們發布了基準、工具、掃描配置和完整的運行語料庫。
2605.00334.author: Ranit Karmakar et.al.
